mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an approaching economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 143000.0 in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and falling housing starts indicates unsustainable leverage-driven consumption, which triggers a mean-reversion correction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a positive direction-accuracy rate above 55% over any 12-month rolling window, indicating the divergence does not predict equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index relative to GDP growth reflects expanding productive capacity that outpaces demand, compressing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO/GDPC1 ratio rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when the ratio threshold is breached, rejecting the margin compression premise.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing positive forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional portfolios adjust to higher fundamental valuations.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return spread between above-trend and below-trend GDP growth periods fails to exceed 0.5% on an annualized basis over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive, suppressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change falls below 2.5%
failure_condition: The real rate threshold fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy greater than 50% in out-of-sample testing, invalidating the restrictive policy premise.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in average monthly payroll gains signals a labor market inflection point, triggering defensive portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp while PAYEMS growth decelerates
regime_hint: labor market peak and subsequent softening
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical instances, disproving the defensive rebalancing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising household credit balances coupled with stable housing activity indicate resilient consumer spending power, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS exceeds 450000.0 and HOUST remains above 1050.0
failure_condition: The combined threshold fails to generate a positive forward return spread exceeding 0.3% over a 3-year rolling window, rejecting the consumer spending premise.
